g.text: CPI and PCE inflation are shown as year-over-year changes; dotted line marks 2%.
5Y breakeven and 5Y5Y forward inflation show whether markets expect inflation to stay anchored.
Unemployment captures slack; payroll momentum shows whether job creation is cooling.
Average hourly earnings growth is shown year over year; dotted line marks 3%.
Read clockwise: realized inflation shows what households and firms have already experienced; market inflation expectations show whether
investors see inflation staying anchored; labor slack and payroll momentum show whether demand for workers is cooling; and wage growth
indicates whether labor-cost pressure remains consistent with a durable disinflation path. FRED inputs: CPIAUCSL, PCEPI, T5YIE, T5YIFR, UNRATE,
